title: Quick n' Cozy Crochet Afghan
description: The Quick n' Cozy Crochet Afghan is the ultimate crochet pattern for beginners. This crochet afghan is so easy, quick and fun to work up. You don't need much experience to make this crochet pattern, and you can crochet it up in a jiffy! Those who are first attempting a beginner crochet project may be hesitant, but this crochet blanket pattern really is incredibly simple.
Yarn Weight: (6) Super Bulky/Super Chunky (4-11 stitches for 4 inches)

title: World's Easiest Afghan
description: If you've been searching for easy afghan patterns, then the crochet blanket pattern you've all been waiting for is finally here! The World's Easiest Afghan is one that anyone can make and use.The texture of the finished afghan makes it look fancy, well-done, and unique even though it only uses single and double crochet.No one even has to know that you were learninghow to crochet a blanketwhile you were working it up, or that it didn't take you very much time at all. This blanket can easily be worked up in multiple sizes and shades.Using bulky yarn, a larger hook, and simple stitches, you can create this blanket in a matter of hours. Print the free pattern below or watch the video.Use the dark, emerald green that's pictured for a warm accent in your living room, or make a bright pop of color to put in your kid's bedroom. Use this video tutorial to show you just how easy it is for you to make this crochet afghan pattern, and use it to decorate your house!Subscribe to our newsletter for amazing crochet patterns delivered to your inbox daily!PLUS!Check out our video tutorial below to help you master this blanket.
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Ribbed Fingerless Gloves
description: "These crochet ribbed fingerless gloves are made with medium-weight yarn and a 4.5 mm hook. The ribbed texture creates a stretchy, comfortable fit that keeps hands warm while leaving fingers free. The gloves are worked flat and then seamed, making the construction simple and beginner-friendly. The seam is nearly invisible, giving the gloves a clean and polished look. This practical and stylish accessory is perfect for everyday wear during chilly autumn and winter days. Happy Crocheting!"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Chunky Adult Slippers
description: Learn how to crochet an adorable pair of Chunky Adult Slippers with this easy to follow tutorial. Single crochet is worked to the finishing touch. Add a button, a flower or even a pom pom to add a fun embellishment to your cute new slipper.
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Quick Little Bag
description: "This Quick Little Bag is just that, and it came about due to a lack of pockets! I was at a trade show and realized most of my outfits for the long weekend had no pockets. I had a big shoulder bag, but my phone and business cards kept getting lost in it. So I grabbed some Red Heart Unforgettable and a crochet hook and whipped up this bag. Problem solved! I made a new sample for the pattern that is a little thinner, longer, and has a flap. Enjoy."
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Berry Delight Free Crochet Beanie Pattern
description: The Berry Delight Beanie is a crochet hat pattern that is worked up with bulky yarn, so it's fast to make. This crochet beanie features an easy stitch pattern that is fun to crochet and lends a nice texture to the finished fabric. Stay cozy this winter with this easy pattern and crochet one for every member of your family. This pattern is written to include sizes preemie through large adult, so no one will be left out. The hat is worked from the brim up to the top of the hat, and then the ribbing is worked separately and then seamed at the end.
Yarn Weight: (5) Bulky/Chunky (12-15 stitches for 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: Gauge in HDC: 11 stitches and 9 rows = 4" (10 cm)
Notes: -Hat is worked from the brim up; the brim (ribbing) is worked in rows and seamed together at the end -Starting chain does not count as a stitch unless directed otherwise -Each round is started in the same stitch as your starting chain -The end of each round is joined by slip stitching in the first stitch of the round unless directed otherwise

title: Glamorous Pearl Center Flowers
description: This pattern is so short and sweet, you will definitely do a double-take. The Glamorous Pearl Center Flowers are inspired by hydrangeas and the rich purple colors of their interlocking petals that can form lovely round bouquets. These simple crochet flowers get their extra pop from adding a pearl center to the four-petal design. These centers could be adjusted to a smaller, purple bead to look more like hydrangeas, or you can keep the large white pearl to really make a glamorous statement. Whatever route you choose, the crochet flower pattern will be a delightfully easy project for anyone craving their floral fix.
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Thick and Bumpy Potholder
description: "This Thick and Bumpy Potholder is bumpy with puff stitches on one side, and ribbed on the other. It is super thick and has a really nice texture. The best part - you only need 1 ball of yarn to make the whole thing!"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Beginner Friendly Crochet Doily
description: "Beginner friendly crochet doily, nice easy pattern for those of you who are just starting to learn to make crochet doilies. A lovely home decor piece with a lacy edge, this doily can also be used as mats for dining, occasional, coffee or side tables or you can use them as gifts for family and friends. Made in light worsted #3 (double knitting for UK users) cotton yarn with a 4.50mm crochet hook size. Hope you have fun making it."
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Bob
description: "It features rows of double crochet interspersed with rows of filet stitches which create ladders throughout the wrap. I hit a wall coming up with a name, so I asked a group of friends, and Drew Emborsky suggested Bob. I’m pretty sure he was joking, but it was the only suggestion I got, and it made me laugh, so I went with it! So grab your Wool and the Gang Tina Tape and get crocheting!"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Sequin Hearts Wrap
description: Wear your heart on your sleeve-- or rather your back-- with this gorgeous Sequin Hearts Wrap! Use white yarn and sequins to create this wearable crochet pattern. It will add the perfect amount of sparkle to any outfit!
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Griffin Shawl
description: "For this crochet shawl / scarf, I chose Lion Brand Yarns Mandala in Griffin, which is a DK weight, self-striping yarn. All of the colorwork was done for me which made this a perfect crochet project for binge watching my favorite TV shows. And I don’t know about you, but my favorite part about this yarn is that I wasn’t left with a bunch of ends to weave in! This shawl pattern size is easily customizable."
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Butterfly Vibe 12-inch Afghan Square
description: "This beautiful 12″ afghan square is simple, quick to work up, and gorgeous! This square pattern reminds me of a butterfly’s wings so we are calling it the Butterfly Vibe Afghan Square.  If you’re not familiar with afghan squares, what’s great about them is that yes, you can compile a bunch of them into one large blanket, but you can also whip up one for a child’s lovey (I’d just keep adding more rows) or make two of them to form a pillow cover!"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Candy Swirl Shawl
description: "With warm weather and bright spring colors making the world feel extra inspirational lately, this free shawl crochet pattern is perfect for the season! The stripes give you the perfect opportunity to play with color, while the fun swirl adds a unique element. Add a lovely texture to the mix, and you’ll enjoy having this crochet shawl fly off your hook! This free crochet shawl pattern features DK (3) or Light Worsted (4) Weight Yarn and size 6.5mm crochet hook."
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: 8 sts and 8 rows = 4 inches in Single Crochet

title: Lisa Wrap
description: "The Lisa Wrap was inspired by my Fins Wrap, and can be made to any size you would like, you are only limited by how much yarn you have! The sample was made with 1 full skein of Lisa Souza Hardtwist, 100% Superwash Merino. Buy a second skein for a nice long wrap, or stay with just one skein for a pretty, smaller wrap as seen in the sample. So grab your hook and get creating!"
Yarn Weight: (2) Fine (23-26 stitches to 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: 17 sts x 9 rows = 4” [10 cm] in dc

title: Sargassum
description: "Capture the feeling of delicate, lacy strands of seaweed, white sand beaches littered with shells, and the sparkle of sunshine on the clear, blue ocean. Sargassum is the name of a seaweed that washes ashore on the gulf beaches of Florida, where I recently went for a vacation. The days were full of warm sunshine, but there was still enough of a chill in the early mornings and late evenings to make this shawl a welcome accessory. The striping pattern gently shifts from more green to more blue, without repeating, while optional beads add a nice weight, and occasional sparkle as they catch the sunlight."
Yarn Weight: (2) Fine (23-26 stitches to 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: 17 dc x 7 rows = 4 inches (after blocking). Exact gauge is not critical.

title: One Skein Lilac Blanket
description: This One Skein Lilac Blanket is the best free crochet pattern to make for any little baby in your life. Beautiful light purple yarn makes this an excellent choice for a spring baby, but you're welcome to use any color Caron One Pound yarn for this particular crocheted afghan. The texture and timeless design of this baby blanket makes this a classic item that will be cherished for years to come. Plus, it works up quickly, making this a wonderful choice for holiday gifts and baby showers.
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: 3 clusters and 6 rows = 4" [10 cm] in pat

title: Say a Little Prayer Shawl
description: The Say a Little Prayer Shawl from Red Heart is an easy crochet pattern to make for someone that need a little extra comfort and support in their life. If you or someone you know is struggling either mentally, emotionally, or physically, then this free crochet shawl is just what the doctor ordered. It will not only provide warmth to anyone that wears it, but also comfort and love. It's a truly heartwarming feeling to know that this cozy crochet shawl was made specifically for you by someone that loves you.
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Sand Dollar Dream Afghan
description:
Make this Sand Dollar Dream Afghan if you're looking to brighten up your home.  The beautiful star design will make you feel like you're relaxing under the evening sky or surfing the waves at the beach. This free crochet afghan pattern by Bernat is an intermediate crochet pattern to complete. Use your two favorite contrasting colors to complete this homemade blanket, or choose two colors that match your home decor. It measures approximately 44 by 54 inches when complete. Use your two favorite contrasting colors to complete this homemade blanket, or choose two colors that match your home decor. It measures approximately 44 by 54 inches when complete.

Thanks to imjstagrl 6312434 for the great name suggestion! You all had amazing suggestions!
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Shamrock Granny Square
description:
"Easy to follow crochet pattern of 10x10cm/4x4" square with 3D shamrock motif.
Use very small amounts of DK yarn (10g for each square!) to create unique squares, that you can combine with other square motif and make crochet design of your dreams. Make a blanket, bag, sweater or bunting to celebrate st Patrick's Day!
There's a row by row stitch count for each square.

Skills required (US terms):
-crocheting in rows
-crocheting in rounds
-single crochet
-half double crochet

Pattern is written in US terms, in ENGLISH ONLY."
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)

title: The Shell Fold Napkin Edging | Free Crochet Pattern
description:
"There’s something quietly beautiful about the small details that bring a table together—the kind that feel effortless, yet completely intentional.

This crochet-edged linen napkin was inspired by a moment in Chapter 2 of The Sea Glass Journal, where a simple folding technique transforms an ordinary cloth into something sculptural and refined. With just a few folds and a delicate shell edging, the napkin settles into shape naturally—soft, balanced, and unexpectedly elegant.

The edging is worked in a classic shell pattern using cotton yarn, adding texture and a subtle decorative finish without overwhelming the simplicity of the linen. Paired with an accordion-style fold and a napkin ring, it becomes a beautiful detail for everyday dining or special occasions alike.

Whether you’re setting a table for guests or simply elevating a quiet meal at home, this design is a reminder that the smallest creative touches can make a lasting impression.

The Story Behind the Design

In Chapter 2 of The Sea Glass Journal, the task seems simple—prepare the table. But like so many moments in the story, it’s the quiet transformation of something ordinary that stands out.

“She wanted cloth—folded. Something sculptural…”

With a few deliberate movements, the napkin becomes more than functional. It becomes part of the atmosphere—something thoughtful, something beautiful, something just right.

This pattern was created to capture that feeling.

If you’d like to experience the full moment that inspired this design, you can watch Chapter 2 here:"
Yarn Weight: (1) Super Fine (27-32 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Frost Blossom Cowl Crochet Pattern
description:
"Wrap yourself in cozy texture with the Frost Blossom Cowl, a beautifully simple crochet pattern designed for comfort, style, and everyday wear.
This cowl is part of the Frost Blossom Collection, a special collaboration between Vicky and her daughter Casey. Using the same stitch and yarn combination as the rest of the collection, this piece brings that signature “frost meets bloom” look into a wearable accessory you’ll reach for all season long.
The pattern features the lemon peel stitch—a relaxing combination of single and double crochet stitches that creates a soft, textured fabric with a woven look. It’s easy to learn, enjoyable to repeat, and perfect for crocheters of all skill levels.
This cowl is worked flat in rows and then seamed, making construction simple while still giving you a polished finished piece.
"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Lilienne Poncho
description:
"The Lilienne Poncho is a lightweight rectangle poncho that’s simple to crochet and easy to wear.

Worked in one piece with an airy mesh yoke and textured crossed stitches, it creates a beautiful drape while staying light and breathable.

The design can be worn two ways, giving you a choice of neckline styles with the same piece.

One size fits most body types. Front and back panels measure 38 inches each (76 inches total circumference).

An easy, go-to piece that feels just as lovely to wear as it is to crochet.

/DETAILS
Skill level : Beginner Friendly
US Terminology

/MATERIALS
4mm / G-6 Crochet Hook
Cotton DK (#3 weight) yarn; 1205 yards
Stitch Markers
Tape Measure
Scissors
Needle

/FINISHED SIZE
Each panel measures approx. 38 inches / 97cm (W) X 20 inches / 51 cm (L)

Front and back panels: 38 inches each (76 inches total circumference)

/STITCHES AND ABBREVIATIONS USED
Single crochet (sc)
Double crochet (dc)
Crossed Double Crochet (Xdc)
Slip stitch (sl st)

Chain/s (ch/s)
Continue (cont)
Each (ea)
Previous (prev)
Repeat (rep)
Right Side (RS)
Skip (sk)
Space (sp)
Stitch (st)"
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)
Finished Size: One Size Front and back panels: 38 inches each (76 inches total circumference)
Crochet Gauge: 14 stitches and 10 rows = 4” x 4” Pattern used for gauge swatch:  1 row double crochet, 1 row crossed double crochet alternating 5 times

title: Small Alligator
description:
"Introducing the Small Crochet Alligator! This cutie is quick to make and full of personality, featuring the option to make him all one color or add a colored tummy in the color of your choosing. Its compact size makes it ideal for using up leftover yarn while creating something irresistibly cute.

Whether you keep it as a miniature plush or attach it to a keychain to carry a bit of handmade happiness wherever you go, this little alligator is sure to bring smiles. With simple shaping and playful details, it’s a fun project that works up quickly and makes a delightful addition to any crochet collection, a gift for someone, or to be sold at craft fairs. 
PATTERN INFORMATION
+ The pattern is written in English using US crochet terminology.

SKILLS NEEDED
+ The skill level needed is advanced beginner.  
+ The techniques you will need to know include chain, single crochet, slip stitch, single crochet increase and decrease, invisible decrease, working in the back bar of the ch, bobble stitch, starting an oval piece with a foundation chain, and the adjustable (magic) ring. 

MATERIALS NEEDED (more specifics are noted within the pattern)
+ yarn in your choice of colors (about 44 yds/40 m of worsted/aran)
+ a size E/4 (3.5 mm) crochet hook
+ stitch markers 
+ tapestry needle 
+ stuffing
+ pins
+ scissors 
+ safety eyes
+ chopstick to help stuff

FINISHED MEASUREMENTS
+ Using hook size E/4 (3.5 mm) and worsted yarn, the Small Crochet Alligator measures approx. 3.25 inches (8.25 cm) tall. 
+ Using hook size 1.50 mm and size 10 crochet thread, the Miniature Small Crochet Alligator measures approx. 1.5 inches (3.5 cm) tall.
"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: Gauge is not essential to this project, but a tight, even tension should be maintained.

title: Sweet Berry Continuous Granny Square Baby Blanket
description:
"An easy continuous granny square baby blanket made with soft cotton yarn. Perfect for stroller rides, car seats, and cozy snuggles.
This is a simple and enjoyable pattern that’s perfect for beginners. It’s a great project for making a crochet baby blanket, car seat blanket, crib blanket, throw, or lapghan.
It’s a great way to learn how to crochet a classic granny square blanket. The pattern includes clear, step-by-step written instructions, and if you ever feel unsure or need a little extra guidance, there’s a full video tutorial that walks you through every step of making your blanket.
Weekend Crochet Project
"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Princess Buttercup Crochet Dress Pattern
description:
"Free Crochet Sundress Pattern: Princess Buttercup Lace Sundress
A breezy crochet sundress made from delicate floral strips and flattering wedge panels that create an elegant A-line silhouette. The adjustable corset-style back allows for a customizable fit, making this dress perfect as a summer outfit or a stylish beach cover-up.

This lightweight crochet dress works beautifully in cotton or bamboo yarns and can easily transition from daytime beachwear to evening resort style.

The pattern is available in 3 loose size ranges and has a customizable fit with a corset tie in the back. The pattern features #3 dk weight yarn and offers many suggested yarns in a variety of fibers and budgets."
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)
Finished Size: Small (32–34″ bust) Medium (36–38″ bust) Large (40–42″ bust)  The corset-style back allows for adjustable fit across a range of body shapes.
Crochet Gauge: 1 Flower Strip = 4″ (10 cm) wide, after blocking 6 rows stitch pattern (one repeat) = 2 3/4″ [7 cm] long, after blocking 16 dc = 4″ (10 cm) Take time to check gauge.

title: How To Front Loop Double Crochet
description:
"Do you want to learn how to front loop double crochet (FLdc)? If so, this tutorial is just right for you!

This post will cover the basics of front loop double crochet stitches, materials needed, abbreviations and charts symbol, tutorials with photos and video, as well as answering frequently asked questions about this technique. In addition, I’ll provide related posts that you might find useful in mastering this stitch.

Everyone from crochet beginners to experienced crocheters can benefit from learning this versatile crochet stitch which can be used for crochet projects like garments and accessories. It is also great for creating different textures on crochet fabrics. So let’s get started!"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: How To Get A Flat Wrong Side In Overlay Mosaic Crochet With Anchored Double Crochet
description:
"If you want to achieve a flat wrong side in overlay mosaic crochet, then you’re at the right place.

This technique involves crocheting the double crochet stitches a bit different that normal in overlay mosaic crochet & the result is a flat surface at the back instead of uneven ridges.

Having a flat backside on your mosaic crochet projects can be the nice finish you are looking for on your mosaic crochet projects.

The anchored double crochet stitch is just what you need to implement to get those results. Let’s dive into this technique."
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: How To Overlay Mosaic Crochet Without Cutting Yarn
description:
"In this tutorial you’ll learn how to overlay mosaic crochet without cutting yarn.

If you love overlay mosaic crochet, but don’t like all the ends to weave in, then you’ve come to the right place.

While mosaic crochet can be fun & amazing, there’s no secret that the amount of yarn ends to weave in is a bit of a downside. There’s some great techniques that solves that issue, like creating fringe with the ends to weave in or a double layered crocheted border.

But isn’t it better to avoid cutting the yarn at all? Let’s dive into this technique."
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Crochet Phone Pocket
description:
"Create a stylish and practical crochet phone pocket with this easy and beginner-friendly pattern. This crochet cellphone pouch is designed to keep your phone safe while adding a touch of handmade charm to your everyday accessories. With simple stitches and a compact design, it’s a fun project for anyone who loves making useful crochet items. Whether you’re heading out for a quick errand, a walk, or a casual day out, this crochet phone purse keeps your essentials close and organized.

This versatile crochet phone pouch can also work as a small crochet wallet purse or mini bag for carrying cards, cash, earbuds, or other tiny essentials. The lightweight design makes it comfortable to wear, especially when made as a crochet crossbody bag with a long strap. You can easily customize your crochet mobile bag by choosing different yarn colors, adding buttons, tassels, or textured stitches to match your personal style. It’s a great way to turn a simple accessory into something unique and handmade.

If you enjoy making crochet phone accessories, this project is both practical and creative. The crochet phone bag is perfect for everyday use, travel, festivals, or quick trips when you only need the basics. It’s also a wonderful handmade gift idea for friends, teens, and family members who appreciate useful DIY items. Small crochet projects like this are ideal for using leftover yarn while creating something beautiful and functional.

Whether you call it a crochet cellphone pouch, crochet earphone pouch, crochet wallet purse, or crochet phone bag, this pattern is a simple and satisfying make. Add this cute DIY phone pouch to your list of crochet projects and enjoy crafting an accessory you can use every day."
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Crochet V-neck Sweater Pattern
description:
"The Crochet V-neck Sweater Pattern brings a classic twist to the free easy crochet sweater pattern scene, making it a great addition to your crocheted garments.

Using simple, beginner friendly stitches like the half double crochet stitch, this crochet pattern helps you craft a classic silhouette that can be worn for casual outings or dressed up for special occasions.

Make this amazing v-neck sweater part of your wardrobe for a good, modern look with minimal effort.

Find more: 25+ crochet sweater pattern collection."
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)

title: How To Crochet The Woodland Stitch
description:
"Keep reading to learn how to crochet the woodland stitch. This crochet stitch pattern might be known under other names. However like many of my other favorite stitches, I don’t remember where I first came across it. The Woodland stitch gives a lovely texture & quite a “romantic” & traditional crochet look. It is a fan stitch & it is quite easy.

If you have bought one of my crochet patterns, you might wonder how it is done. The pattern always offers written text in addition to charts, but now I will give you a lot of additional explanations. In addition there is a video of how to crochet this stitch on the bottom.

Today I suggest that you learn the stitch & then you should use it in a project! You can make you own project, but on the bottom of this post I have also included all crochet patterns that are using the woodland stitch."
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Tulip Coaster Crochet Pattern
description:
"Bring a touch of spring to your home with this Tulip Coaster Crochet Pattern! These cheerful crochet tulip coasters are made with simple stitches and are quick and enjoyable to make. Once you get the rhythm of the pattern, you can finish a coaster in about 30 minutes, making this a wonderfully satisfying project when you’re in the mood for something quick and creative. The pattern includes clear written instructions along with a step-by-step video tutorial to guide you through the entire process.

Tulips are one of my favorite flowers, and designing these coasters was such a joy. I’ve always loved how tulips signal the arrival of spring after a long winter, and I wanted to create a crochet design that captures that same bright and uplifting feeling.

These coasters are also perfect for using up small bits of scrap yarn from other projects. Since they work up so quickly, it’s easy to crochet several in one sitting and make a whole set. They’re wonderful for gifting, adding a handmade touch to your home, or even making multiples to sell at craft shows."
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Top Deck Tee
description:
"If you’re looking for a fun and easy crochet project for summer, this top-down raglan crochet top is a great one to try. This easy crochet summer top free pattern is beginner friendly and worked in one piece from the neckline down, so there’s no seaming needed at all. The raglan construction creates a comfortable fit across the shoulders, and the simple stitches make it a really relaxing project. The striped crochet top pattern adds a playful pop of colour, but you can easily customise it with your own favourite shades.
This summer top crochet pattern is perfect if you’re making your first crochet garment. Since it’s worked from the top down, you can try it on as you go and adjust the length to suit you. The crochet tee pattern includes sizes XS, S, M, L, XL, 2XL, 3XL, 4XL and 5XL, so you can find the fit that works best. If you’ve been wanting to try making a wearable piece, this beginner friendly crochet pattern is a great place to start."
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: In alternating dc and sc rounds, is 16.5 sts by 13 rounds for a 10 cm by 10 cm / 4” by 4” square.

title: Coastal Breeze Pullover
description:
"The Coastal Breeze Sweater is an easy crochet pullover worked in one piece using double crochet stitches. The pattern includes six sizes and two length options, so you can choose the fit you prefer. Variegated DK yarn with long colour changes work well creating soft stripes across the sweater without needing to change colours. Solid colours work too.

The relaxed design makes it a great project for confident beginners so if you want to try crocheting a sweater without complicated shaping, this design is perfect.

/DETAILS
Skill level : Easy
US Terminology

/MATERIALS
DK yarn (#3 weight) see below for amounts
4mm Crochet Hook
Stitch Markers
Tape Measure
Dressmaker Pins
Scissors
Yarn Needle

/FINISHED SIZE and YARN AMOUNTS
To Fit Chest (inches) Finished Size Width approx. (inches) Waist Length 19 (inches) Yarn (yds) approx. Over Hips Length 23 (inches) Yarn (yds) approx.
S 32 – 34 38 1100 1290
M 36 – 38 42 1202 1412
L 40 – 42 46 1322 1552
XL 44 – 46 50 1442 1695
2XL 48 – 50 54 1563 1836
3XL 52 – 54 58 1683 1980

/STITCHES AND ABBREVIATIONS USED
single crochet (sc)
single crochet back loop only (scblo)
single crochet 2 together (sc2tog)
double crochet (dc)
slip stitch (sl st)

chain/s (ch/s)
each (ea)
repeat (rep)
Right Side (RS)
Stitch/es (st/s)
Wrong Side"
Yarn Weight: (3) Light/DK (21-24 stitches to 4 inches)
Crochet Gauge: With a 4mm hook: 16 double crochet and 10 rows = 10cm x 10cm/4in x 4in

title: Crochet Coaster
description:
"Add a sophisticated flair to your home with the Lacy Shell Cup Coaster. This easy-to-follow pattern uses standard US crochet terminology to create a stunning textured finish.

Skill Level: Beginner-Friendly

Project Type: Home Decor/Kitchen

Materials Needed: Medium (4) Cotton Yarn, 4mm Hook

Finished Size: Perfect for standard mugs and tumblers.
This pattern is ideal for using up leftover cotton yarn from larger projects. Create a cohesive set for your dining room or wrap a few up with a mug for a thoughtful, personalized gift"
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Textured Star Stitch Origami Bag
description: "This Textured Star Stitch Origami Bag is a modern crochet shoulder bag with beautiful texture and a clever construction. The bag is crocheted as one large rectangle using the textured star stitch pattern, then folded origami style and sewn to create the final shape. Made with sturdy cord yarn, the fabric has great structure while still showing off the rich stitch texture. The bag is finished with a simple crochet strap and an optional leather closure. This stylish project is perfect for crocheters who enjoy textured stitches and unique bag designs."
Yarn Weight: (5) Bulky/Chunky (12-15 stitches for 4 inches)
Finished Size: Bag Finished Size (without the strap): approx. 34 cm wide × 18 cm high at the lowest point and 33 cm high at the highest point
Crochet Gauge: 6 stitches and 7 rows = 10 cm in Textured Star Stitch pattern (1 star stitch BLO row + 1 hdc row)

title: A Zig & A Zag Blanket
description:
"The A Zig & A Zag Blanket is a fun "Choose your own adventure" project allowing you to easily work the sections you like in any order you desire.  This blanket is also a great way to learn new stitches and create a fun chevron style stitch blanket.  Easily adjust your blanket to the size you want by adding or leaving out pattern sections to create a one of a kind throw for your home.  

The A Zig & A Zag blanket is also a great option for using up your scrap yarn making it ideal for when you can't make it to the store to pick up more skeins.    "
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Baby Tay 18" Doll Dress
description:
"Whether you're looking to add some fun new dresses to your daughter's doll collection or wanting to make a beautiful blessing dress for a preemie baby, this pattern is a wonderful option!  Using less than a skein of yarn, you're sure to use up some of that yarn stash you have hiding away for just the right project. 

This dress is worked from the neck down and has a skill level of easy.  With a full video tutorial accompanying the written pattern, you're sure to quickly work through a dress in an evening. 

The pattern uses a foundation chain to begin which is shown within the video tutorial. It also includes information on using a basic chain to start if you prefer using that instead of the foundation chain. 

This is one of those projects that "I bet you can't make just one"!!  Trust me, they're addicting!    "
Crochet Hook: G/6 or 4 mm hook, H/8 or 5 mm hook
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)

title: Mini Boba Amigurumi
description:
"If you love tiny, cheerful crafts and can’t resist a good bubble tea, this mini boba tea amigurumi is the perfect project! Fun, colorful, and adorable, these little drinks make great decorations, keychains, or gifts for boba-loving friends!

Whether you are new to amigurumi or a seasoned crocheter looking for a fun, quick project, you’ll love working up your mini boba tea amigurumi and be tempted to keep making them in all the colors!

Enjoy working up this free crochet pattern! Head to the blog post for more details. "
Yarn Weight: (4) Medium Weight/Worsted Weight and Aran (16-20 stitches to 4 inches)
